payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming by children
payload.justification: Claiming represents the strategic process by which children record completion of tasks.

payload.description: Task definition by parents
payload.justification: Task definition is the strategic process parents use to create work items with rewards.

payload.description: Parental review of earnings and completion
payload.justification: Review process enables parents to verify task completion before disbursement.

payload.description: Earnings disbursement to children
payload.justification: Disbursement is the implied payment process following parental review.

payload.description: Pocket money task
payload.justification: Tasks are the core entity representing work items with monetary value that children complete and parents define.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Earnings total is a distinct strategic entity representing accumulated monetary value from completed tasks.

payload.description: Monetary reward associated with task
payload.justification: Monetary reward is a defining attribute of pocket money tasks.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Completion status distinguishes available from completed tasks and is central to claiming and review.

payload.description: Weekly earnings cycle
payload.justification: The week represents the strategic temporal cycle governing task claiming and earnings totaling.

payload.description: Week end occurrence
payload.justification: Week end is the temporal event when earnings totals become visible to children.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Children are distinct actors who claim tasks, view availability, and see earnings.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Parents are distinct actors who define tasks and review completion for payment.

payload.description: Enable children to record completed pocket money work
payload.justification: Core strategic goal of empowering children to claim their completed tasks.

payload.description: Provide task and earnings visibility to children
payload.justification: Strategic goal of transparency enabling children to see available tasks and accumulated earnings.

payload.description: Enable parental control of task rewards and payment verification
payload.justification: Strategic goal empowering parents to define incentives and verify work before payment.

payload.description: Define reusable task with monetary value
payload.justification: Core business capability enabling parents to create task definitions.

payload.description: View available and personally completed tasks
payload.justification: Business capability providing children visibility into task data.

payload.description: View aggregated earnings from completed tasks for settlement
payload.justification: Business capability enabling children to see calculated earnings. | Business capability supporting parent financial settlement process.

payload.description: Track task completion and pocket money allocation between children and parents
payload.justification: Core business process describing the end-to-end workflow of the system.

payload.description: Completed tasks must be associated with a child entity
payload.justification: Business rule establishing mandatory relationship between task completion and child.

payload.description: Task availability state resets on weekly cycle
payload.justification: Business rule defining cyclical reset behavior for task state.

payload.description: Task definition with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes reusable task entities with monetary values as a core business object.

payload.description: Child entity participating in task completion system
payload.justification: Child is a core business entity mentioned in the system concept and task completion relationships.

payload.description: Parent entity managing task definitions and settlements
payload.justification: Parent is a core business entity defining tasks and reviewing earnings for settlement.

payload.description: Completed task instance
payload.justification: Completed tasks are distinct entity instances tracked separately from task definitions.

payload.description: Aggregated earnings from completed tasks
payload.justification: Earnings are calculated business objects derived from task completion data.

payload.description: Association between completed task instance and child entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly establishes this relationship as a business rule.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings relationship for financial settlement
payload.justification: Signal describes consolidated view of three-way relationship between child, tasks, and earnings.

payload.description: Task availability state within weekly period
payload.justification: Task availability is a temporal attribute that resets weekly and affects business operations.

payload.description: Weekly reporting period for task completion and earnings
payload.justification: Weekly time boundary is used for both task visibility and earnings calculation.

payload.description: Weekly reset of task availability state
payload.justification: Weekly cyclical reset is a recurring business event that changes system state.

payload.description: Parent role defining tasks and reviewing settlements
payload.justification: Parent functions as a distinct business role with task definition and oversight responsibilities.

payload.description: Child role completing tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: Child functions as a distinct business role with task completion and earnings visibility.

payload.description: Enable tracking of task completion and pocket money allocation
payload.justification: Core business objective defining the purpose of the system.

payload.description: Historical task completion data must be temporally persistent and accessible
payload.justification: Business requirement constraining system behavior to preserve and provide access to historical data.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines the enterprise accountability for establishing and maintaining the parent-child relationship roles through which household responsibility, delegation, and oversight are expressed.

payload.name: Household Work Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to defining compensable household work units, their associated monetary value, completion criteria, and the periodic earnings structure that governs child compensation.

payload.name: Delegation and Oversight Authority
payload.description: Governs the enterprise mechanisms through which authority to assign work is delegated, work commitments are accepted, completion is verified, and approval authority over disbursements is retained by senior stakeholders.

payload.name: Stakeholder Transparency
payload.description: Ensures the enterprise obligation to provide visibility into work obligations, completion status, and earned compensation to participating stakeholders, supporting informed decision-making and accountability.

payload.name: Obligation Specification Stewardship
payload.description: Responsibility for establishing and maintaining the definitions of reusable obligations (tasks) that carry monetary value, including their availability states and lifecycle governance within operational cycles.

payload.name: Participation Record Accountability
payload.description: Responsibility for recording the completion of obligations by participants, ensuring accurate attribution to individuals, and maintaining the temporal persistence of participation records across the business lifecycle.

payload.name: Financial Settlement Governance
payload.description: Responsibility for accumulating monetary entitlements derived from completed obligations, governing the reporting cycle for settlements, and enabling oversight of participant earnings for settlement purposes.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise child user as a participant role.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to recognising the child user as a distinct role within the family participation structure. This is a structural obligation defining a participant entity at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parent user as a participant role.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to recognising the parent user as a distinct role within the family participation structure. This is a structural obligation defining a participant entity at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish household work units eligible for child compensation.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to defining the scope of compensable household work as discrete tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a monetary value for each compensable household work unit.
payload.rationale: Each task entity carries an associated monetary reward attribute that the enterprise must establish and maintain.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish completion criteria for household work units.
payload.rationale: The enterprise must define what constitutes a completed task to enable determination of compensation eligibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide aggregate child earnings on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to calculating and presenting total compensation at week-end occurrence, establishing the temporal structure of the earnings obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish mechanisms by which children may claim tasks defined by parents.
payload.rationale: CCIs describe a bidirectional enterprise process: parents define tasks and children claim them. This is a single delegation capability enabling enterprise task assignment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain oversight of task completion and associated earnings through parental review.
payload.rationale: Parental review of earnings and completion represents an enterprise accountability mechanism for verifying delegated work.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall retain authority over the disbursement of earnings to children.
payload.rationale: Disbursement authority is retained at the parental level, representing an enterprise governance constraint aligned with the goal of parental control over rewards and payment verification.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable participating stakeholders to record completed work obligations.
payload.rationale: Derived from the enterprise goal to enable children to record completed pocket money work. At Row 1, this is expressed as the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for stakeholders to record work completion, without specifying system mechanics.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into work obligations and earned compensation to participating stakeholders.
payload.rationale: Derived from the enterprise goal to provide task and earnings visibility. This expresses the enterprise commitment to transparency regarding work obligations and compensation, supporting the stakeholder transparency domain.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a definition for each reusable obligation that specifies its associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: HOW-column capability to define reusable tasks and WHAT-column entity describing task definitions with monetary value both express the business responsibility to maintain obligation specifications with their compensation amounts.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain an availability state for each obligation within the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column attribute expressing that each task carries an availability state scoped to a weekly period. This is a business artefact the business must maintain to govern which obligations are available for assignment.

payload.statement: The business shall reset the availability state of each obligation at the commencement of each weekly operational cycle.
payload.rationale: HOW-column rule describing weekly reset behaviour and WHEN-column event marking the weekly reset trigger together express a business capability to govern obligation availability on a periodic basis.

payload.statement: The parent role shall be accountable for establishing obligation definitions and reviewing compensation settlements.
payload.rationale: WHO-column role describing the parent's responsibilities for task definition and settlement review. This expresses the structural association between the parent role and two key business accountabilities within obligation stewardship.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to each child participant the set of tasks personally completed by that participant.
payload.rationale: Derived from the business capability to view personally completed tasks, scoped to the child role acting as participant. This is a business responsibility to make information available, not a system display function.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to each child participant the tasks available for completion.
payload.rationale: Derived from the viewing capability for available tasks. Distinct from completed tasks; separated under the two-step 'and' test as no single obligation subsumes both viewing available and viewing completed.

payload.statement: The business shall account for task completion by each child participant.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to account for task completion events. 'Account for' is business-level vocabulary expressing the capability to track without implying technical storage or processing.

payload.statement: The business shall account for pocket money allocation to each child participant.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for pocket money allocation, distinct from task completion accounting. Separated as two responsibilities under the two-step test.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce association of each completed task instance with a child entity.
payload.rationale: WHY-column rule (presented as How-column type=Rule) establishing a mandatory business constraint on task completion attribution. The business must enforce this association.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the child as a participant role responsible for completing tasks and viewing earnings.
payload.rationale: Structural obligation to recognise the child participant role within the business. WHO-column role combined with WHAT-column entity defining the participant structure.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completed task instance.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining completed task records with temporal persistence. 'Maintain a record' expresses business accountability without technical storage vocabulary.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical task completion data remains accessible across the business lifecycle without time-bound expiration.

payload.statement: A completed task instance shall be associated with exactly one child entity.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion of the mandatory one-to-one relationship between completed task and child. WHAT-column relationship defining business entity structure.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce temporal persistence of historical task completion records.
payload.rationale: WHY-column constraint mandating that historical data remain persistent and accessible. This is a governance rule the business must enforce, not a technical retention policy.
payload.fit_criteria: Task completion records remain accessible for the duration of participant engagement and beyond, without degradation or loss.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of aggregated earnings for each participant derived from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column entity (aggregated earnings) and HOW-column function (view aggregated earnings) together establish the business responsibility to maintain and make available participant earnings records derived from task completion.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain the relationship between each participant and their task-derived earnings for settlement purposes.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column relationship CCI explicitly defines the child-task-earnings association that must be maintained for financial settlement — a structural obligation at the business level.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a parent role accountable for defining task obligations and reviewing settlements.
payload.rationale: WHO-column role and WHAT-column parent entity together establish the structural requirement for the business to recognise a parent role with authority over task definitions and settlement review.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a child role accountable for completing tasks and viewing their earnings.
payload.rationale: WHO-column role establishes the structural requirement for the business to recognise a child participant role with responsibility for task completion and earnings visibility.

payload.statement: The business shall govern the reporting cycle for task completion and earnings on a weekly basis.
payload.rationale: WHEN-column cycle establishes a temporal governance constraint on the business reporting obligation — a weekly cadence for settlement-related reporting.
payload.fit_criteria: Reporting cycle interval equals seven calendar days.

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for enabling oversight of task completion and participant earnings allocation.
payload.rationale: WHY-column goal establishes the business capability to enable tracking and allocation visibility — a functional responsibility at the business-owner level.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks throughout the week.
Articulates parental motivation to define tasks with associated monetary rewards.
Expresses the intent to provide children visibility into available and completed tasks.
States the purpose of enabling children to see weekly earnings totals at week end.
Articulates parental need to review child earnings and task completion for disbursement purposes.
Document section marker indicating the beginning of conceptual system overview content.
Core business concept defining a system for tracking task completion and pocket money allocation between children and parents.
Business capability enabling parents to define reusable task entities with associated monetary values.
Business rule establishing a relationship between completed task instances and child entities.
Business capability providing children visibility into both available and personally completed task entities within a weekly time boundary.
Business capability enabling children to view aggregated earnings calculated from completed tasks within a weekly reporting period.
Business capability providing parents a consolidated view of child-task-earnings relationships to support financial settlement.
Business rule defining weekly cyclical reset behavior for task availability state.
Business requirement for temporal persistence and accessibility of historical task completion data.
